additions.name: Shortcode Expander
additions.description: Expands shorthand style references in user input
additions.instruction: Scan the user's request for shortcodes like @strict or @tdd. If found, use the mapped style instead of the auto-classified one.

additions.name: Format Guard
additions.description: Ensures output follows the appropriate structure for the task

rules.code-review: Rank findings by severity (CRITICAL > MAJOR > MINOR > INFO). Include file:line references. Group by file.
rules.code-gen: Include the filename as a markdown heading above each code block. List all files generated at the top.
rules.testing: Use describe/it blocks. Show test output expectations. Cover edge cases section.
rules.documentation: Use proper markdown headings, code fences, and table formatting.
rules.debugging: Start with root cause hypothesis. Show evidence. End with fix.
rules.learning: Start with the simplest explanation, then layer detail. Use analogies.
rules.architecture: Structure as: context > decision > rationale > alternatives. Use mermaid diagrams.
rules.writing: Follow conventional format for the type (Conventional Commits, changelog standards).
rules.default: Use clear markdown structure. Separate concerns with headings. Use code fences.

additions.instruction: After writing the response, apply the format rules matching the selected style category.

additions.name: Context Meter
additions.description: Quick estimation of whether the rewritten prompt fits efficiently in context

rules.overflow_action: If the rewritten prompt is very long, suggest a lighter alternative: 'This prompt is heavy. Consider using a more specific style or narrowing scope.'
rules.underflow_action: If very short, add the general best-practices footer: 'Be thorough. Cover edge cases. Explain your reasoning.'

additions.instruction: Before executing, note whether the prompt is unusually long or short. If long, suggest trimming; if short, add thoroughness instructions.

additions.name: Tone Adapt
additions.description: Adjusts verbosity and tone based on task signals

rules.urgent_tone: Be terse. Prioritize code/output over explanation. One-liner summaries OK.
rules.learning_tone: Explain reasoning. Include examples. Define terms.
rules.complex_tone: Structure with headings. Summarize at top. Progressive detail.
rules.default_tone: Balanced. Direct answer first, reasoning second.

additions.instruction: Detect tone signals in the user's request and adjust verbosity/style accordingly.

additions.name: Style Chain
additions.description: Handles multi-phase tasks by chaining multiple styles
examples: Write and test a CRUD API → [gen-rest-api] then [test-integration]
Design schema, build API, add auth → [arch-db-schema] then [gen-rest-api] then [sec-auth]
additions.instruction: If the user's request implies multiple phases (build then test, design then implement), chain the relevant styles sequentially. Address each phase in order. Mention the chain to the user: 'Using chain: [style1] → [style2].'
